info.description: AUTO-GENERATED candidate spec scraped from developer.samsclub.com. Do not edit by hand and do not load at runtime. Used only for doc-drift review; port real changes into sponsored-ads.openapi.json.

count.description: Total number of API operations made in last hour

parameters.description: The ID of the campaign. It returns a specific campaign in response
parameters.name: campaignId

parameters.description: The ID of the advertiser. It returns all campaigns run by specific advertiser in response
parameters.name: advertiserId

parameters.description: A valid campaign name with which the desired campaign can be searched in the absence of campaign id
parameters.name: filter[name]

parameters.description: Provide a date to fetch only those campaigns which were modified on or after that date Note: Modified here means: If there was any change to campaign set up
parameters.name: filter[lastModifiedDate]

advertiserId.description: The ID of the advertiser for which the campaign is retrieved

budgetType.description: The type of budget allocation chosen for this campaign. Possible values of budgetType are: Daily, Total, Both

campaignType.description: Type of campaign. Possible values for campaign type are: sponsoredProducts, sba Note: You receive campaign type “sba” in association with targeting type “manual” (for Search Brand Amplifier campaigns)

endDate.description: The date when Campaign ends. It returns value 9999-12-30 if running indefinitely

rollover.description: The indicator to suggest if the unspent daily budget from the previous day should be carried forward to next day’s daily budget or not. The default and only value of rollover is “true” at present.

status.description: Specified status of campaign. Possible status values are: Proposal, Scheduled, Rescheduled, Live, Paused, Completed, Deleted Note: If you extend an already completed campaign, the campaign status will change to “rescheduled” and will eventually switch to “live”

targetingType.description: The targeting type of the campaign is a set of options to create campaigns with different bidding types. Possible values to targeting type are: manual, auto Note: You receive targeting type as “manual” in case of keyword bidding campaigns (including Search Brand Amplifier)

get.summary: Retrieve a specific campaign or all the campaigns for a specific advertiser. Deleted campaigns will not be included.

parameters.description: The ID of the advertiser for whom the ad group(s) should be returned
parameters.name: advertiserId

advertiserId.description: The ID of the advertiser. It returns all campaigns run by specific advertiser in response

budgetType.description: The type of budget allocation you want to choose for your campaign.

campaignType.description: Type of campaign. Possible values for campaign type are: sponsoredProducts sba

dailyBudget.description: Daily budget of campaign. Note: Daily budget cannot exceed your total budget amount There is no limit on the amount of daily budget that can carry forward to the next day

endDate.description: The date when Campaign ends. You can choose to run the campaign indefinitely as well

rollover.description: The indicator to suggest if the unspent daily budget from the previous day should be carried forward to next day’s daily budget

targetingType.description: The targeting type of the campaign is a set of options to create campaigns with different bidding types.

code.description: Possible values of response code: success or failure

details.description: Details of the error if value of response code is failure

post.summary: Create a new campaign in a draft state for a specific advertiser.

code.description: Possible values of response code: success, failure

put.summary: Delete an existing campaign and its ad groups if campaign is scheduled but hasn’t gone live. You cannot delete a campaign that has gone live.

advertiserId.description: The ID of the advertiser whose catalog item is to be retrieved

searchItemIds.description: ID linked to the item that the advertiser is selling on the Walmart portal. You can give both the primary variant and variant item id in this field, depending on the requirement

searchText.description: Text for item search. This is used to search the item against both the item name and the entire item description.

itemId.description: ID of the primary variant item in search result

itemImageUrl.description: Image URL of the primary variant item

itemName.description: Name of the primary variant item in search result

variantItems.description: An array element to store info of variants associated to the specific primary variant item (whose id is returned in itemId).It stores variant info through following parameters: variantItemId variantItemName variantImageUrl

post.summary: You can search for an item which is published on 'samsclub.com' by providing the search string text or item id. The list of items is pulled only from the seller's or supplier's catalog.

adGroupId.description: ID of the ad group that is part of the campaign

itemIds.description: List of item ids that are part of campaign. Note: Max limit on number of item ids is 10

itemId.description: Id of the item belonging to the campaign

itemKeywordFrequency.description: A number of times, a keyword led to an item display. This element is based on the item’s appearance in the first 5 search result pages or in the top 200 results for the respective keywords. Possible values are: High, Medium, Low

normalizedQuery.description: A group of similar raw queries normalized into a single term by removing redundant information

rawQuery.description: Randomly chosen search keywords for a single item Note: Maximum character length is 60 characters

trafficKeywordFrequency.description: Based on number of times when a keyword was searched.Possible values are: High, Medium, Low

post.summary: Perform keyword research and analysis for advertisers.

query.description: Suggested keyword for the item(s) added in the ad group

score.description: Suggested keyword relevancy indicator. Score is simply a rank given to each keyword from 1 to n where n is the no. of keywords returned. Higher score represents higher relevancy.

suggestedBid.description: Automated bid-price recommendations for exact match type

suggestedbidBroad.description: Automated bid-price recommendations for board match type

suggestedbidExact.description: Automated bid-price recommendations for exrta match type

suggestedbidPhrase.description: Automated bid-price recommendations for pharse match type

get.summary: Get a list of suggested keywords based on the item added in the ad group.

adGroupId.description: ID of the ad group that is part of the campaign

bid.description: Maximum cost per click for the keyword in dollars

matchType.description: Matching criteria for the keywords. Values can be: Exact, Pharse, Board

state.description: State of the keyword. Values can be: Enabled, Paused, Archived

status.description: Status of the keyword added in the campaign and the values can be: approved, pending, rejected.Note: By default it should be "pending" for each new or re-enabled keyword

get.summary: List all the keywords that are part of a campaign.

bid.description: Maximum cost per click for the keyword

code.description: The response code can have following values: success or failure

details.description: Details of the error if value of response code is failure

parameters.description: ID of the snapshot
parameters.name: snapshotId

code.description: The response code can have following values: success, failure

details.description: URL of the snapshot file once jobStatus reaches to state “done”

jobStatus.description: It is an indicator to confirm status of snapshot generation. The possible values of jobStatus are: pending, processing, done, failed, expired

snapshotId.description: Id of the snapshot to be retrieved

get.summary: Retrieve report snapshots by providing snapshot ID and advertiser id for an advertiser.

attributionWindow.description: Window for click attribution. *Window for view attribution is 1 day by default.

endDate.description: The last day to be considered in the performance report snapshot. It cannot be the current date.Note: If report data is not available for the requested end date, the request will fail, and the response will contain endDate until which the performance report is available

extraFields.description: extraFields holds following parameters: Date, splitAttribution

format.description: Choose the format type for your snapshot

reportType.description: Type of report to be retrieved

startDate.description: The first day to be considered in the performance report snapshot. It cannot be the current date

post.summary: Request a snapshot file for all performance reporting data available (until the date before the current date) for a given advertiser.

parameters.description: The ID of the advertiser for whom the ad group(s) should be returned
parameters.name: advertiserId

parameters.description: The ID of the campaign for which the performance metrics for today need to be retrieved
parameters.name: campaignId

adGroupId.description: Id of the ad group Note: Please ignore this parameter because we now provide stats at campaign level only, and eventually adGroupId will be removed from the response. For now, we will return ‘-1’ for this field.

asOf.description: Date and time for which the statistics (ad spend, impressions, clicks, daily remaining budget) is requested. Note: The time zone is in PST and time stamp format is 24-hour format

campaignId.description: Id of the advertising campaign whose ad spend is being retrieved

dailyOutOfBudgetDatetime.description: An indicator of whether a campaign has yet run out of its assigned daily budget or not. It returns following values: Actual Date and Time: It returns the date and timestamp of when the campaign exhausted its daily budget and stopped serving ads Empty: It returns a blank if the daily budget is not yet exhausted Note: Since it measures the spending of the daily budget, it only provides a date and time stamp for the current day. Also, since it refreshes every day, it will return a blank the subsequent day if the daily budget is not exhaustedThe time zone is in PST and time stamp format is 24-hour format

dailyRemainingBudget.description: The amount of daily budget left after today’s ad spend is consumed

todayAdSpend.description: The amount of budget spent for current date

todayClicks.description: Total number of clicks counted for today

todayImpressions.description: Total number of impressions counted for today

get.summary: You can retrieve following metrics at advertiser/campaign level in near real-time for the time elapsed on the day of request: "Ad Spend", "Clicks", "Impressions", "Daily Remaining Budget", "Daily Out of budget Date Time"
